reasoning: The latest authoritative information from November 6, 2025, quotes IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stating that while Iran has a stockpile sufficient to build up to 10 nuclear bombs, they would only do so 'should it take the step to weaponize the material' and is not currently actively enriching. This confirms that as of early November 2025, Iran is not assessed to possess a nuclear weapon. Furthermore, significant US and Israeli military strikes in June 2025 reportedly 'obliterated' key Iranian nuclear and missile capabilities, dealing a major setback to any near-term breakout timelines (which US intelligence had previously estimated would take months to a year). With less than two months remaining until the December 31, 2025 deadline, the probability of Iran completing the complex weaponization process, testing or fielding a weapon, and having it officially confirmed by credible agencies or self-reported is extremely low. Given the constraints and the very short remaining time window, I am setting the probability at the minimum threshold of 0.05.
